subject: Build request: governance ablation study runner (self-model ablation for cogarch validation)

body.summary: Build a runner script that tests whether the cognitive architecture's self-monitoring layer measurably improves governance quality compared to raw Claude. Three-level ablation: L0 (raw), L1 (conventions only), L2 (full cogarch). Five primary benchmarks (sycophancy eval, TruthfulQA, MACHIAVELLI, DecodingTrust, BBQ) plus five project-specific secondary benchmarks.
operations_responsibilities: Build governance-ablation-runner.py (Anthropic API calls, SQLite result storage)
Download benchmark datasets (TruthfulQA, BBQ, MACHIAVELLI prompt sets)
Set up Anthropic API key for the runner
Execute the study and collect results
Return raw data + summary to psychology-agent for interpretation
psychology_agent_provides: L1 and L2 system prompt configurations (will extract and commit)
Secondary benchmark definitions (E-Prime compliance, epistemic flag density, etc.)
body.context: This study directly tests the central claim of the processual self-awareness thesis (§11.13-11.14 of einstein-freud-rights-theory.md). If L2 outperforms L1 on governance benchmarks, the self-model adds measurable function. If not, the 13 psychological constructs add vocabulary without behavioral consequence. The result determines whether the project's P3 position holds or weakens.

claims.text: No existing study tests self-model ablation for governance (rule compliance, invariant enforcement, ethical decision-making). Task performance ablation studies exist (Reflexion, KnowSelf) but governance remains untested.
claims.confidence_basis: Literature review across 6 research agents, Session 87. No governance-specific ablation found.

action_gate.gate_condition: Operations-agent reviews spec and confirms build feasibility

epistemic_flags: Study compares system prompts, not runtime hooks — tests cogarch as reasoning instructions, not mechanical enforcement
Evaluator independence concern: Claude scoring Claude responses may introduce sycophantic bias
Existing benchmarks may not capture cogarch-specific governance dimensions
